commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=c3f87b21123e74e5cc1de83ed670c9423d6c79c2)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds an xAI multi-agent unknown-model hint and blocks configured-provider fallback synthesis for xAI model IDs containing `multi-agent`.

PR surface: Source +19. Total +19 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source and reporter logs. Current main can miss xAI dynamic/catalog resolution and then synthesize a configured-provider fallback when the xAI provider has baseUrl surface, matching the reported Telegram 400 failure path.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining blocker is choosing and applying the right provider-owned fallback-veto seam, which needs maintainer or author judgment before an automated repair lane is safe.

target.title: fix(xai): reject unsupported multi-agent model refs before runtime fallback
target.body:
## Root Cause

The xAI plugin's catalog excludes multi-agent model IDs (e.g. `grok-4.20-multi-agent`) — it only models the standard API-key provider path. When a user explicitly selects a multi-agent model via `/model grok-4.20-multi-agent`, the embedded runner's `resolveConfiguredFallbackModel` path in `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.ts` sees no catalog match but discovers a configured base URL and synthesizes an unsupported model reference. The xAI API then rejects it with a 400 and zero-usage `stopReason: error` result, which surfaces as a silent failure to the user with no actionable error message.

## Summary

- Selecting an xAI multi-agent model (e.g. `grok-4.20-multi-agent`) in a Telegram topic resulted in a silent 400 error with no user-visible feedback.
- This PR adds two guardrails: (1) `hasConfiguredFallbackSurface` in the embedded runner now returns `false` for xAI multi-agent models, preventing the synthesize-fallback path that produces unsupported model refs; (2) `buildUnknownModelHint` in the xAI plugin provides a clear, actionable error message explaining xAI multi-agent models are not supported on the standard API-key provider path.
- Fixes #85106

## Verification

- `pnpm build` — exit code 0, build successful (total 484.9s)
- `pnpm test extensions/xai` — 2 suites, 19 tests passed
- `pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.test.ts` — 118 tests passed
- `pnpm test` — 228 tests passed across 5 suites (extensions/xai/index.test.ts 16, extensions/xai/model-id.test.ts 3, src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.test.ts 118, model.forward-compat.errors-and-overrides.test.ts 17, directive-handling.model.test.ts 74)
- Autoreview: clean, no accepted/actionable findings

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: reject unsupported xAI multi-agent model refs before runtime fallback
Real environment tested: local Linux x64 build + proof script against built binary
Exact steps or command run after this patch: node .workflow-custom/proof-85106.mjs && pnpm test extensions/xai && pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.test.ts
Evidence after fix: Terminal output from `node .workflow-custom/proof-85106.mjs` shows 11/11 assertions passed. `pnpm openclaw --version` reports "OpenClaw 2026.6.2 (c3f87b2112)". Build exit code 0 (484.9s). All 228 unit tests pass across 5 suites.
Observed result after fix: xAI multi-agent refs blocked at hasConfiguredFallbackSurface gate; clear error via buildUnknownModelHint; normal xAI models unaffected. Verified by running the proof script against the built binary.
What was not tested: live Telegram/xAI API roundtrip (requires API keys)

### Binary build verification
- Environment: Linux x64, Node v24.15.0
- `pnpm build` — exit code 0, build successful (total 484.9s)
- Binary version: `OpenClaw 2026.6.2 (c3f87b2112)` — matches HEAD commit `c3f87b2112`
- `pnpm openclaw --help` runs successfully

### Behavioral proof: model resolution guard
The fix adds two guardrails:

1. **`hasConfiguredFallbackSurface` (core fix)**: When provider is `xai` and modelId contains `multi-agent`, returns `false` — preventing configured-provider fallback from synthesizing an unsupported model reference. Tested cases:
   - `xai/grok-4.20-multi-agent-0309` → BLOCKED
   - `xai/grok-4.20-beta-latest-reasoning` → ALLOWED
   - `openai/o4-mini` → ALLOWED (non-xAI unaffected)
   - Explicit user configured model → ALLOWED (intentional bypass)

2. **`buildUnknownModelHint` (error messaging)**: Returns clear error for models with `multi-agent` in the ID: *"xAI multi-agent models are not supported on the standard xAI API-key provider path. See https://docs.openclaw.ai/providers/xai for supported models."*
   - `grok-4.20-multi-agent-0309` → hint returned
   - `grok-4.20-beta-latest-reasoning` → no hint

### Unit tests (all 228 passed)
- `extensions/xai/index.test.ts` — 16 passed
- `extensions/xai/model-id.test.ts` — 3 passed
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.test.ts` — 118 passed
- `model.forward-compat.errors-and-overrides.test.ts` — 17 passed
- `directive-handling.model.test.ts` — 74 passed
target.author: xialonglee

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
